title: Airlock Contract
description: Schema for an Airlock contract: a self-declared disclosure of how an AI-agent harness handles inbound traffic and what it can/can't do internally. v0.3 reframes the contract from a service-RPC manifest into a full harness-capability disclosure. See CONTEXT.md and docs/adr/ for the canonical terminology and decisions. Per ADR 0004, blocks are tagged in their descriptions as 'binding' (load-bearing promise) or 'informational' (deployment fact, may change in minor versions).

airlock.description: Airlock spec version this contract conforms to. v0.3 is the current major; v0.1 and v0.2 are not accepted (see docs/migration-v01-to-v03.md).

schemas.description: Reusable type definitions referenced by skills and tools via $ref.

skills.description: BINDING. The set of skills this agent exposes to consumers. Each skill is one named external interaction. Consumers call skills; the harness may reach for tools to fulfill them.

tools.description: BINDING. Discrete capabilities the harness invokes internally during its work (e.g. read_file, bash, web_fetch). Distinct from skills. Authority rules and permissions may target tools.

hooks.description: BINDING. Lifecycle interception points the harness fires. Each hook's mode (observe/mutate/block) is load-bearing — consumers reason about blast radius differently for a silent observer than for a mutating gate.

mcp_servers.description: INFORMATIONAL. Model Context Protocol servers the harness loads to source tools from. Tools sourced via MCP carry a `source: {kind: 'mcp', server: '<name>'}` discriminator. Per ADR 0004, swapping MCP servers does not require a major bump as long as the tools surface is preserved.

secrets.description: INFORMATIONAL. Named env-vars or credentials the harness reads. Names and purposes only — never values. Lets consumers reason about blast radius.

delegates_to.description: INFORMATIONAL. Other Airlock contract URLs this agent may dispatch sub-work to. A transitive trust surface; consumers fetch the linked contracts and reason about the chain.

authority.description: BINDING. Declarative rules describing what the agent will do unilaterally vs. refuse vs. escalate. Each rule self-classifies its binding_class and targets either a skill or a tool.

instant_failures.description: BINDING. Conditions that reject the request on sight, before any work. Always produce PROMISE codes.

sla.description: BINDING. Per-skill or per-tool response time commitments. Keys may be a bare skill id (back-compat shorthand for `skill:<id>`), or `skill:<id>` / `tool:<id>` explicitly.

name.description: Stable identifier for the agent. Recommend kebab-case.

version.description: Contract version. SemVer. Major bumps require a deprecation block on the prior major.

description.description: Human and LLM-readable summary of what the agent does.

channels.description: Channels this contract is served over. v1 ships only the http adapter.

homepage.description: Publisher's landing page or product URL (for the rendered docs).

Harness.description: INFORMATIONAL. Runtime envelope serving this contract. Per ADR 0004, changes here do not require a major bump.

TypeDef.description: A JSON-Schema-compatible type definition. Airlock embeds JSON Schema as the type system.

id.description: Unique identifier for the skill. Recommend snake_case.

input.description: JSON Schema for the input payload. Can be inline or a $ref into #/schemas.

output.description: JSON Schema for the output payload on success.

examples.description: Worked examples. Used by the renderer (humans), the sandbox (replay), and the conformance runner (test fixtures). When no example matches a verdict, the sandbox falls back to the schema-derived faker (see ADR 0005).

Tool.description: A capability the harness invokes during its work. Differs from a Skill: skills are external (consumers call them); tools are internal (the harness reaches for them).

input_schema.description: JSON Schema for tool invocation arguments. Inline or $ref into #/schemas.

output_schema.description: JSON Schema for tool output. The faker uses this when synthesising tool-call envelopes.

side_effects.description: Effects this tool may have. Closed enum for v0.3.

source.description: Where this tool comes from. Omit for built-in tools.

server.description: When kind=mcp, the name of the MCP server (must match an entry in mcp_servers[]).

mode.description: observe = read-only audit. mutate = may rewrite the in-flight payload. block = may halt the action. Mode is binding; consumers reason about blast radius based on it.

skill.description: Optional: scope this hook to a specific skill id. Omit to apply to all.

tool.description: Optional: scope this hook to a specific tool id. Only meaningful for pre_tool_use/post_tool_use events.

endpoint.description: MCP server URL or local command. Informational only.

allowed_tools.description: Subset whitelist of the server's tools the harness will actually expose. If omitted, all tools from this server are exposed.

Permissions.description: BINDING. Static allow/disallow entries against typed resources. Per ADR 0004, the resource taxonomy is a closed v0.3 enum; unknown resources are linted as warnings.

PermissionEntry.description: Either a structured object {resource, op, scope} or its short-form string 'resource.op:scope' (e.g. 'fs.read:./src/**'). Loader canonicalizes strings to the object form.

op.description: Operation specific to the resource (e.g. 'read', 'write' for fs; 'connect' for network; the verb for tool/mcp; 'read' for env/secret).

scope.description: Resource-specific scope grammar — globs for fs, hostnames/CIDRs for network, tool ids for tool, server names for mcp, var names for env/secret.

Guardrails.description: BINDING. Categorical refusals at the agent level (coarser than authority rules).

refused_topics.description: Topic strings the agent will not engage with (e.g. 'financial-advice', 'medical-diagnosis').

refused_actions.description: Named actions the agent will not perform (e.g. 'commit-secrets', 'force-push-main', 'delete-production-data').

required_authentication.description: If true, the agent refuses unauthenticated requests categorically.

name.description: Env-var or credential name. Never the value.

purpose.description: What the harness uses this secret for.

ExpectedVerdict.description: What pre-flight should return for this example input. The conformance runner uses this as an assertion when the example's code is a PROMISE.

ref.description: Optional pointer to the rule expected to produce this verdict.

AuthorityRule.description: Targets exactly one of `skill` or `tool` via the oneOf constraint below.

id.description: Stable identifier; referenced by `ref` in verdicts.

skill.description: Skill id this rule applies to. Mutually exclusive with `tool`.

tool.description: Tool id this rule applies to. Mutually exclusive with `skill`. The expression language exposes `tool.<field>` references against the tool's input_schema.

field.description: Optional: the input field the rule operates on (for docs rendering).

binding_class.description: Whether this rule's verdicts are PROMISE (deterministic) or ESTIMATE (judgment). The validator lints: deterministic rules may not reference runtime state.

when.description: Expression evaluated against the input (or tool args). Tiny safe expression language: comparisons, arithmetic, logical, field refs, abs/min/max/len, regex match.

InstantFailure.description: Conditions that reject on sight. Always PROMISE — they must be deterministic by construction.

skill.description: Optional: scope this failure to a specific skill. Omit to apply to all skills.

when.description: Expression evaluated against the input (same tiny safe language as authority rules).

code.description: Must be a Phase 1 or Phase 2 code (identification or input validation).

Actions.description: Catalog of foreign actions this agent may take in real responses.

respond_within.description: Duration string (e.g. "5m", "24h", "3d"). Pre-flight to terminal lifecycle state.

on_breach.description: What happens if the SLA is breached.

states.description: Lifecycle states this agent uses. Subset of Phase 5 codes.

Deprecation.description: When set, this contract version is being phased out. Pre-flight against this version returns OUT_OF_SCOPE with the redirect in detail.

replaced_by_url.description: URL of the contract that replaces this one.

sunset.description: Date after which this contract is no longer honored.

StatusCode.description: All Airlock status codes across the 6 phases. Phases 1–3 are PROMISE; phase 4 is ESTIMATE; phases 5–6 are real-response only.

ActionCode.description: Phase 6 action codes — what the agent actually did in a real response.
